items.prompt_md: The running `tank` goes negative at index `i`, having started tracking from candidate start `s`. What does the exchange argument let you conclude about every station strictly between `s` and `i`?
options: None of them can be a valid start either, so the next candidate to try is `i + 1`
One of them might still work, so each must be tested individually
The gas and cost arrays must be re-sorted before continuing
The scan must restart from index 0 with `tank` and `total` both reset
items.answer: None of them can be a valid start either, so the next candidate to try is `i + 1`
items.explanation_md: Each station between `s` and `i` had a nonnegative running total measured from `s` — otherwise the tank would have failed there first. Starting at any of them throws away that surplus, so they fail by `i` too. The whole block is dominated and can be skipped at once.

items.prompt_md: You must find a single starting index from which a running balance of gains minus costs, taken around a full circuit, never goes negative. Which approach fits in one O(n) pass?
options: Binary search over candidate start indices
Try every start index and simulate the full circuit for each, an O(n^2) check
Scan once, resetting the candidate start (and running tank) to the next index whenever the tank dips negative
Sort stations by gain and greedily take from the largest surplus first
items.answer: Scan once, resetting the candidate start (and running tank) to the next index whenever the tank dips negative
items.explanation_md: A failed candidate proves every station up to the failure point is dominated and can't work either, so one reset-on-negative pass replaces testing each candidate from scratch.

items.prompt_md: What is the time and space complexity of the one-pass reset-on-negative-tank solution to Gas Station?
options: O(n log n) time, O(n) space
items.explanation_md: One left-to-right scan updating three running numbers (`total`, `tank`, `start`) — no per-station retry and no auxiliary array, so it's O(n) time and O(1) extra space.
